name: Banks of the Eria and Órbigo Trail
name_original: Riberas del Eria y del Órbigo — circular desde Morales de Rey
description: Hike a moderate 8.3-mile (13.4 km) circular route from Morales de Rey, exploring the banks of the Eria and Órbigo rivers.
region_intro: Hiking around Morales De Rey offers routes through varied landscapes, characterized by river valleys, gentle hills, and agricultural plains. The region features trails along the banks of the Eria and Órbigo rivers, providing flat paths with water views. Higher ground includes areas like the Sierra de las Labradas, offering slightly more elevation and panoramic vistas. The terrain generally consists of accessible paths suitable for exploring the local natural and historical sites. Best hiking trails around Morales De Rey The most popular hiking route is Basilia's Balcony and Coto, a 4.9 miles (7.8 km) trail that takes 2 hours 13 minutes to complete, offering moderate ascents and varied scenery. Another top favourite among local hikers is Banks of the Eria and Órbigo Trail, a moderate 8.3 miles (13.4 km) path. This route follows the riverbanks, providing flat terrain and open views, typically completed in about 3 hours 23 minutes. Local hikers also love the Peña Redonda and miradores del Coto and de las Suertes — circular by the Valles de Benavente from Morales de Rey, an 8.5 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through the Valles de Benavente, often completed in about 3 hours 43 minutes. Hiking around Morales De Rey is defined by river valleys, agricultural landscapes, and gentle hills, with options for both easy and moderate ability levels. The trails often feature historical elements like dolmens and castles. The routes in Morales De Rey are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.0 stars from more than 1 review. More than 50 hikers have used komoot to explore Morales De Rey's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Morales De Rey?
A: There are &lt;em&gt;over 15 hiking routes&lt;/em&gt; in the Morales De Rey area, ranging from easy strolls to more challenging treks. In total, komoot lists 17 routes for you to explore.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Morales De Rey?
A: Yes, Morales De Rey offers &lt;em&gt;7 easy hiking routes&lt;/em&gt;. These trails are generally flat and follow riverbanks or agricultural paths, perfect for a relaxed walk. They are ideal for those new to hiking or looking for a less strenuous experience.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Morales De Rey?
A: Yes, several circular routes are available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022509543/pena-redonda-and-miradores-del-coto-and-de-las-suertes-circular-by-the-valles-de-benavente-from-morales-de-rey" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Redonda and miradores del Coto and de las Suertes — circular by the Valles de Benavente from Morales de Rey&lt;/a&gt; offers an 8.5-mile loop through varied landscapes. Another option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305549898/arrabalde-circular-through-the-sierra-de-las-labradas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arrabalde — circular through the Sierra de las Labradas&lt;/a&gt;, which is 5.2 miles long.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect to see on hikes around Morales De Rey?
A: Hiking around Morales De Rey is characterized by diverse landscapes including river valleys, gentle hills, and agricultural plains. You'll find paths along the banks of the Eria and Órbigo rivers, as well as routes through higher ground like the Sierra de las Labradas, offering panoramic vistas.

Q: Are there any historical sites or natural monuments to see along the trails?
A: Yes, the region is rich in historical and natural points of interest. You can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5966274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de San Adrián&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5453005" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro de Las Labradas&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1305829771/route-of-the-dolmens-and-castles-of-granucillo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Dolmens and Castles of Granucillo&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights these ancient structures.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Morales De Rey?
A: The best time for hiking in Morales De Rey is typically during spring and autumn when the weather is mild and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be warm, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable, while winters are generally cooler but still offer opportunities for hiking.

Q: Are there any castles or notable settlements to visit near the hiking routes?
A: Absolutely. The area features several historical castles and charming settlements. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5786385" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de los Pimentel&lt;/a&gt; or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5509500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente Castle – Tower of the Snail&lt;/a&gt;. The town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2720898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benavente&lt;/a&gt; itself is also a significant point of interest.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Morales De Rey?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.0 stars. Hikers often praise the varied scenery, from tranquil riverbanks to gentle hills, and the opportunity to discover historical elements like dolmens and castles along the paths.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Morales De Rey suitable for families with children?
A: Many of the easier routes along the riverbanks or through agricultural areas are suitable for families. These paths often have minimal elevation gain and offer pleasant, open views, making them enjoyable for all ages. Consider the 7 easy routes available for family outings.

Q: Is there public transport available to reach the hiking trailheads?
A: Public transport options to specific trailheads in Morales De Rey may be limited. It is generally recommended to use a car for greater flexibility in accessing the various starting points of the hiking routes in the region.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking trails in Morales De Rey?
A: Parking is typically available in or near the villages and towns that serve as starting points for the trails, such as Morales De Rey itself. Specific parking information for individual routes can often be found in the route descriptions on komoot.
tips: The Molino de Abajo, in addition to producing flour and feed, generated electricity to illuminate Morales del Rey. The abundant vegetation of the riverside forest means that this jewel is reserved for explorers and adventurers who decide to delve into the memory of the territory.
At this point we can turn off the path to approach the Vecilla de la Polvorosa Dam. From the Vecilla Dam, the water from the Órbigo river to the Eria is diverted.
Near the path, we come across the Los Linos dam, from where water was derived to irrigate the plain in the last century. A nice picture near Morales de Rey.

faqs.question: Where does the Banks of the Eria and Orbigo - circular from Morales de Rey trail start, and is there parking?
faqs.answer: The trail officially begins and ends at the Church Square in Morales del Rey. Parking is generally available in the village, particularly around the Church Square, which serves as the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The route features a mix of streets within the villages and paths that traverse the fertile agricultural plain formed by the Eria and Órbigo rivers. You'll follow the course of the Eria River, encountering abundant riverside forest vegetation and its frequent meanders.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Banks of the Eria and Orbigo circular hike?
faqs.answer: This trail is considered to be of moderate difficulty. With a distance of approximately 13.4 kilometers and a modest elevation gain of about 28 meters, it's suitable for most hikers looking for an accessible route.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable features, including the Puente Grande (Large Bridge), the historic Molino de Abajo (Lower Mill), and the Puente Colgante (Hanging Bridge). The route also passes by the Presa de Vecilla de la Polvorosa and a fluvial beach in Vecilla de la Polvorosa, which is a great spot for a rest.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, with its easy to moderate difficulty and relatively flat terrain, the trail is generally suitable for families and beginners. The modest elevation gain makes it accessible for a wide range of fitness levels.

faqs.question: Is the Banks of the Eria and Orbigo circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near agricultural areas, to ensure safety and respect for local wildlife and property.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to hike is typically in spring or autumn when temperatures are mild and the riverside vegetation is lush. In winter and early spring, the Eria River can be forceful, while in summer, its bed becomes more exposed, and temperatures can be quite high.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through the towns of Morales del Rey, Vecilla de la Polvorosa, and Fresno de la Polvorosa. You may find local establishments in these villages where you can get refreshments or a meal, particularly in Morales del Rey where the trail starts and ends.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits required or entrance fees to hike the Banks of the Eria and Orbigo circular trail. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with any other hiking paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, a small section of the route, approximately 3%, overlaps with the official Camino Astorgano.

faqs.question: What should I bring for the Banks of the Eria and Orbigo circular hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's length and potential for sun exposure, it's advisable to bring sufficient water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and comfortable walking shoes. Depending on the season, a light jacket might be useful, and insect repellent could be beneficial near the river.

waypoints.name: Presa de Vecilla de la Polvorosa
